failure_surface: SyntaxError on line 1 with invalid character '<tool_call>' at the start of every code block, preventing any spreadsheet manipulation from executing.
root_cause: Agent repeatedly submitted code blocks prefixed with special markup characters (<tool_call>) that are not valid Python syntax. These appear to be internal agent formatting tokens mistakenly included in the executable code payload, causing immediate parsing failure before any spreadsheet logic could run.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Ensure all executable code blocks contain only valid Python syntax without embedded special characters, markup tokens, or formatting symbols that originate from the agent's internal communication protocol.
evidence: All 4 conversation attempts show identical error: "Cell In[1], line 1 ^ SyntaxError: invalid syntax" with caret pointing to '<tool_call>' character
Code content itself (openpyxl logic) was syntactically correct once isolated from the leading characters
Agent iterated through logical variations but never corrected the fundamental code submission format issue
No validation of generated code occurred before execution attempts

memory_items.title: Valid Code Block Submission
memory_items.lesson: Only transmit syntactically valid Python code to execution environments; exclude all internal markup tokens, thinking indicators, or protocol-specific characters.
memory_items.generalization: Before sending any code for execution, validate that it begins with a valid Python keyword or import statement and contains no non-Python characters in the executable payload.

memory_items.title: Pre-Execution Syntax Validation
memory_items.lesson: Perform syntax validation on generated code before attempting execution to catch formatting errors early.
memory_items.generalization: When generating executable code, include a compilation check step to verify Python syntax validity prior to submission to the execution environment.

memory_items.title: Iterative Fix Strategy
memory_items.lesson: When encountering repeated identical errors across multiple attempts, diagnose the common structural issue rather than varying logic parameters.
memory_items.generalization: If the same error type persists across multiple iterations, prioritize fixing the shared artifact (code format, submission structure) over modifying algorithmic details.

edits.content:
## Execution Hygiene
- **Valid Syntax Only**: Ensure all code transmitted to execution environments contains only valid Python syntax without internal markup tokens, special characters, or protocol-specific formatting symbols.
- **Pre-Validation**: Before execution, confirm code blocks begin with valid Python keywords (import, def, class, variable assignment) and compile without syntax errors.
- **Error Pattern Recognition**: When identical errors repeat across multiple attempts, diagnose the shared structural issue in the code submission format before varying algorithmic logic.
